name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise AI platform, orchestrating 80 billion+ enterprise workflows annually across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance. The Now Platform connects systems, data, and processes with AI to eliminate friction in enterprise operations. Following a 5:1 stock split in December 2025, ServiceNow serves over 8,100 customers including 85% of the Fortune 500, generating ~$14B in TTM revenue with Q1 2026 subscription revenues of $3.67B growing 22% YoY. Founded in 2004, headquartered in Santa Clara, California.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has transformed from an ITSM ticketing system (2004-2015) to an enterprise workflow platform (2015-2020) to the leading enterprise AI platform (2020-present) — multiple successful pivots that expanded TAM from ~$20B to $220B+ [1][3]. Each transformation built on the previous while redefining the category. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist generative AI suite net new ACV more than doubled YoY in Q1 2026, with 244 transactions exceeding $1M in net new ACV; Now Assist customers spending >$1M ACV grew over 130% YoY — specific, measurable AI deployment at massive scale [1]. Market Share (2/2): Undisputed category leader in enterprise workflow orchestration with no close independent competitor; 85% of Fortune 500 as customers, cRPO of $12.64B growing 22.5% YoY, and 16 deals over $5M net new ACV in Q1 (up ~80% YoY) [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Armis cybersecurity acquisition expected to close H2 2026 adds security TAM; Moveworks AI customer service integration is live; agentic AI capabilities expanding, but pipeline timelines are general rather than specific. R&D Intensity (1/1): R&D spend is substantial and above enterprise software industry average, supporting the AI platform transformation.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's mission — 'make the world of work, work better for people' — is specific, thoughtful, and genuinely connected to removing workplace friction so humans can focus on higher-value work [3]. CEO McDermott has consistently articulated this purpose across multiple economic cycles and competitive environments. It differentiates from generic software platitudes by focusing on the human experience of work. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow.org provides platform access to nonprofits and the company has committed to net zero by 2030 with SBTi-approved science-based targets, qualifying for general community impact [5]. Employee Retention (0/2): No specific tenure or turnover data publicly available for ServiceNow's 29,187 employees; McDermott stated AI will replace backfilling open roles, which could raise employee concerns [3]. Insider Buying (2/2): CEO McDermott purchased $3M of NOW shares personally in March 2026 during the software sector sell-off, and extended his contract to 2030 — strong personal financial conviction in the long-term story [3].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored as growth stock. Q1 2026 subscription revenues of $3.67B grew 22% YoY with cRPO of $12.64B growing 22.5%, and FY2026 guidance of $15.53-15.57B in subscription revenue at 32% non-GAAP operating margin [1]. D/E of 20.7% scores at ceiling (1.0). ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target (1.0, 2x weight). Revenue growth of 22.1% YoY and ~22% 3yr CAGR both score at ceiling (1.0 each). Forward P/E of 24.7x scores 0.88 — elevated but manageable for a high-quality compounder. Profit margin of 12.6% GAAP scores 0.63, impacted by stock-based compensation. Forward PEG of 1.05 scores 0.98 (2x weight), indicating reasonable growth-adjusted valuation post-correction. Free cash flow of $5.1B TTM represents a 37% FCF margin, demonstrating exceptional cash generation [4].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): ServiceNow's core thesis is that AI creates more demand for enterprise workflow orchestration, not less. CEO McDermott has publicly argued that 'AI excels at identifying problems and providing advice but cannot execute solutions' — ServiceNow controls the execution layer [3]. The company orchestrates 80B+ enterprise workflows annually, and AI agents need orchestration infrastructure to function in complex enterprise environments with regulatory, compliance, and audit requirements. The platform's deep integration into customers' operational fabric (85% of Fortune 500) creates massive switching costs. However, the long-term risk that agentic AI systems mature to replace rather than require workflow orchestration platforms is real and has been flagged by some analysts [3]. AI Opportunity (1/1): Now Assist generative AI suite net new ACV more than doubled YoY in Q1 2026 with 244 transactions >$1M ACV and >130% growth in $1M+ customers — AI is generating substantial, quantifiable incremental revenue [1].

infinite_game.summary: Bill McDermott is a rare enterprise software CEO who operates with explicitly infinite thinking. His 'McDermott Playbook' — organic innovation over dilutive acquisitions, Rule of 55+ profitability standard (revenue growth + FCF margin), personal $3M share purchase during the sector sell-off, and contract extension to 2030 — reflects genuine long-term platform commitment. ServiceNow's Just Cause of removing workplace friction is validated by consistent platform expansion across three transformative pivots over two decades.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): ServiceNow's cause — making work work better for people by eliminating enterprise friction — is genuine, service-oriented, and specific [3]. It describes a future state (frictionless enterprise workflows) that doesn't fully exist yet and invites technology partners and customers to contribute. McDermott's consistent articulation across economic cycles and competitive environments, combined with the platform's evolution through three major pivots, validates the cause through execution. Not yet a full 3 as the company hasn't been tested through a genuine existential crisis.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (2/2): ServiceNow maintains one of the highest CEO approval ratings in enterprise software (consistently above 90% on Glassdoor), and McDermott's 'human-AI' narrative — framing AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them — creates organizational alignment [3]. However, McDermott also stated 'as you have attrition, you don't have to backfill it' regarding AI productivity, which introduces some tension between the empowerment narrative and the cost discipline reality [3]. Leadership continuity under McDermott since 2019 with contract extended to 2030 provides stability.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): McDermott publicly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP, and Microsoft as worthy rivals whose integrations and capabilities push ServiceNow to expand its platform [3]. The Moveworks and Armis acquisitions directly address competitive gaps revealed by rivals' strengths in AI customer service and cybersecurity. McDermott frames competition as motivation to build a better platform rather than a war to win.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The three-phase transformation — ITSM ticketing (2004-2015) to enterprise platform (2015-2020) to enterprise AI platform (2020-present) — is textbook existential flexibility [3]. Each pivot expanded TAM 10x while building on existing customer relationships. The company didn't defend its ITSM monopoly; it redefined the category. The current AI pivot, including the $1B+ Moveworks acquisition and Armis deal, demonstrates ongoing willingness to transform.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): McDermott's $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software sector rout — when the stock dropped on AI disruption fears — is precisely Sinek's definition of courage: acting in service of the Just Cause at personal financial cost [3]. The Rule of 55+ standard (revenue growth + FCF margin must exceed 55%) maintains long-term capital discipline even when it constrains short-term growth. His public pushback against AI disruption fears, arguing ServiceNow is the 'execution layer' that AI requires, demonstrates intellectual courage in the face of market skepticism.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the enterprise AI platform that benefits from — rather than is threatened by — the AI boom. Q1 2026 subscription revenues of $3.67B grew 22% YoY with cRPO of $12.64B (up 22.5%), and FY2026 guidance of $15.53-15.57B at 32% non-GAAP operating margin demonstrates simultaneous growth acceleration and margin expansion [1]. Now Assist AI suite net new ACV more than doubled YoY with 244 deals >$1M, and $1M+ AI customers grew 130% — AI is an additive revenue driver, not a threat [1]. At $124 post-split (down 41% from 52-week highs), the stock trades at 24.7x forward earnings with PEG of 1.05, offering the most attractive entry point in years for a best-in-class SaaS compounder with $5.1B in FCF and 37% FCF margin [4]. CEO McDermott's $3M personal purchase and 2030 contract extension signal maximum conviction [3].
thesis_continued: The primary risk is that ServiceNow's valuation, even after the 41% correction, still prices in sustained 20%+ growth and margin expansion for many years. The January 2026 software sector sell-off exposed a genuine market concern: if agentic AI matures faster than McDermott's 'execution layer' thesis anticipates, enterprise workflows could be replaced rather than orchestrated by AI systems [3]. GAAP profit margin of 12.6% (impacted by ~$3B+ in annual stock-based compensation) raises questions about true profitability. McDermott's statement that AI productivity will replace hiring backfills, while honest, could undermine the employee empowerment narrative if execution becomes visibly cost-focused [3]. The Armis cybersecurity acquisition adds integration risk at a premium valuation. 16 deals >$5M net new ACV (up ~80% YoY) show strong enterprise momentum, but also concentration risk in mega-deals.
key_catalysts: Now Assist AI suite scaling beyond the doubling of net new ACV — continued >100% growth in $1M+ AI customers would validate the AI-additive thesis and justify premium valuation
Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing H2 2026, expanding platform TAM into security operations and demonstrating M&A integration capability
Rule of 55+ standard maintained through FY2026 (20% subscription growth + 37% FCF margin = 57%) validating that AI opportunity is additive to platform economics
key_risks: Agentic AI systems maturing to replace enterprise workflow orchestration within 2-3 years, faster than McDermott's 'execution layer' thesis anticipates — the January 2026 sell-off showed market sensitivity to this narrative
GAAP profitability obscured by ~$3B+ annual stock-based compensation — if SBC growth outpaces revenue growth, true earnings power may be lower than non-GAAP metrics suggest
Mega-deal concentration (16 deals >$5M net new ACV) creates quarter-to-quarter revenue lumpiness and customer concentration risk if any large customers churn
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 Earnings Release — subscription revenue $3.67B, cRPO $12.64B, FY2026 guidance, May 2026
ServiceNow Q4 2025 and FY2025 Financial Results Press Release, January 2026
